chief_complaint: A 23-year-old male presented with weakness starting in his legs that progressed to generalized weakness and body aches.
history: The patient had no past medical history and presented to the emergency department via EMS. Initial concerns were for Guillain-Barre or a tickborne illness.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are provided, but the patient developed ventricular tachycardia that degenerated into ventricular fibrillation.
labs: No specific laboratory results are provided, but the patient had recurrent ventricular arrhythmias and was profoundly hypoxic.
